title: Transcript Coverage Analysis from Long Reads
author: David A Eccles
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.re2d3ge

steps.title: LAST Debian GNU/Linux http://last
steps.instruction: LAST Debian GNU/Linux http://last.cbrc.jp/last/ Martin Frith lastdb Mus_musculus.GRCm38.cds.all.fa Create the transcriptome index from the transcriptome fasta file using lastdb. An anonymous pipe is used

steps.title: Map the reads to the Mmus transcripts with LAST
steps.instruction: Map the reads to the Mmus transcripts with LAST. The results of that mapping can be piped through last-map-probs to exclude unlikely hits, then through my maf_bcsplit.pl script to convert to a one-line-per-mapping CSV format. This CSV format is further processed to make sure that there is only one mapping per transcript-read pair, and then aggregated to sum up counts per transcript. for id in trnCounts_LAST_${id}_vs_Mmus_transcriptome.txt done" description="LAST mapping; probable hit filtering, read counting, and conversion to count file" os_version="" os_name="" guid="1B6502BF437946C3A38477D61796DC83" > for id in "fwd_4T1_BC06" "rev_4T1_BC06" "fwd_4T1_BC07" "rev_4T1_BC07" do lastal Mus_musculus.GRCm38.cds.all.fa <(pv ${id}.correctedReads.uniqueOnly.fasta.gz | zcat) | \ last-map-probs | ~/scripts/maf_bcsplit.pl | awk -F',' '{print $1,$2,$3}' | sort | \ uniq | awk '{print $2,$3}' | sort | uniq -c > trnCounts_LAST_${id}_vs_Mmus_transcriptome.txt done LAST mapping; probable hit filtering, read counting, and conversion to count file

steps.title: Transcript counts are converted into a...
steps.instruction: Transcript counts are converted into a narrow table (one line per transcript/count/barcode tuple) that includes strand direction and a barcode tag: trnCounts <- lapply(c("fwd_4T1_BC06", "rev_4T1_BC06", "fwd_4T1_BC07", "rev_4T1_BC07"), function(x){ res <- read.table(sprintf(("trnCounts_%s_vs_Mmus_transcriptome.txt"), x), stringsAsFactors=FALSE, col.names=c("Count","Transcript", "Direction")); if(grepl("rev",x)){ res$Direction = c("-" = "+", "+" = "-")[res$Direction]; } res$DB <- paste0(sub("..._4T1_","",x), res$Direction); return(res); }); trnCounts <- rbind(trnCounts[[1]],trnCounts[[2]],trnCounts[[3]],trnCounts[[4]]); trnCounts <- lapply(c("fwd_4T1_BC06", "rev_4T1_BC06", "fwd_4T1_BC07", "rev_4T1_BC07"), function(x){ res <- read.table(sprintf(("trnCounts_%s_vs_Mmus_transcriptome.txt"), x), stringsAsFactors=FALSE, col.names=c("Count","Transcript", "Direction")); if(grepl("rev",x)){ res$Direction = c("-" = "+", "+" = "-")[res$Direction]; } res$DB <- paste0(sub("..._4T1_","",x), res$Direction); return(res); }); trnCounts <- rbind(trnCounts[[1]],trnCounts[[2]],trnCounts[[3]],trnCounts[[4]]);

steps.title: The transcript revision number (if any)...
steps.instruction: The transcript revision number (if any) is removed from the transcript ID: trnCounts$Transcript <- sub("\\.[0-9]+$","",trnCounts$Transcript); trnCounts$Transcript <- sub("\\.[0-9]+$","",trnCounts$Transcript);

steps.title: The annotation file is loaded into memory
steps.instruction: The annotation file is loaded into memory: ensembl.df <- read.delim("ensembl_mm10_geneFeatureLocations.txt.gz", col.names=c("Transcript","Description","Start", "End","Strand","Gene","Chr"), stringsAsFactors=FALSE); ensembl.df <- read.delim("ensembl_mm10_geneFeatureLocations.txt.gz", col.names=c("Transcript","Description","Start", "End","Strand","Gene","Chr"), stringsAsFactors=FALSE);

steps.title: The dplyr and tidyr packages are...
steps.instruction: The dplyr and tidyr packages are used to convert to a wide format, and pull the associated annotation from the ensembl annotation file: library(dplyr); library(tidyr); trnCounts.wide <- group_by(trnCounts, DB, Transcript) %>% summarise(Count = sum(Count)) %>% spread(DB, Count) %>% inner_join(ensembl.df, by="Transcript"); library(dplyr); library(tidyr); trnCounts.wide <- group_by(trnCounts, DB, Transcript) %>% summarise(Count = sum(Count)) %>% spread(DB, Count) %>% inner_join(ensembl.df, by="Transcript");

steps.title: Missing data is set to a...
steps.instruction: Missing data is set to a count of zero to simplify subsequent computation: trnCounts.wide$WTfwd <- coalesce(trnCounts.wide$`BC06+`,0L); trnCounts.wide$WTrev <- coalesce(trnCounts.wide$`BC06-`,0L); trnCounts.wide$ρ0fwd <- coalesce(trnCounts.wide$`BC07+`,0L); trnCounts.wide$ρ0rev <- coalesce(trnCounts.wide$`BC07-`,0L); trnCounts.wide$WTfwd <- coalesce(trnCounts.wide$`BC06+`,0L); trnCounts.wide$WTrev <- coalesce(trnCounts.wide$`BC06-`,0L); trnCounts.wide$ρ0fwd <- coalesce(trnCounts.wide$`BC07+`,0L); trnCounts.wide$ρ0rev <- coalesce(trnCounts.wide$`BC07-`,0L);

steps.title: This was a pilot experiment with...
steps.instruction: This was a pilot experiment with no replicate data (hence why I refer to this as 'differential coverage', rather than differential expression. I would usually use software with proper statistical modeling (such as DESeq2 ), but in this case I'm generating a quick rough-cut comparison using basic R. I introduce a fudge factor to account for missing data, and use this factor in the calculation of differential coverage (as log2 fold change). The strand-specificity of the sequencing and transcript annotation allows this differential coverage to be determined for only those reads mapping in the correct direction. Results are rounded to 1 d.p. as an additional warning that these results have a low precision. As the alignment is to transcripts, the direction of the gene is ignored: fcFudge <- 5; trnCounts.wide$DCov <- round(log2(trnCounts.wide$WTfwd+fcFudge)-log2(trnCounts.wide$ρ0fwd+fcFudge),1); fcFudge <- 5; trnCounts.wide$DCov <- round(log2(trnCounts.wide$WTfwd+fcFudge)-log2(trnCounts.wide$ρ0fwd+fcFudge),1); However, if aligning to genome sequences, use the following code: fcFudge <- 5; trnCounts.wide$DCov <- round(ifelse(trnCounts.wide$Strand == 1, log2(trnCounts.wide$WTfwd+fcFudge)-log2(trnCounts.wide$ρ0fwd+fcFudge), log2(trnCounts.wide$WTrev+fcFudge)-log2(trnCounts.wide$ρ0rev+fcFudge)),1); fcFudge <- 5; trnCounts.wide$DCov <- round(ifelse(trnCounts.wide$Strand == 1, log2(trnCounts.wide$WTfwd+fcFudge)-log2(trnCounts.wide$ρ0fwd+fcFudge), log2(trnCounts.wide$WTrev+fcFudge)-log2(trnCounts.wide$ρ0rev+fcFudge)),1);

steps.title: Finally, the resultant table is writtent...
steps.instruction: Finally, the resultant table is writtent out to a CSV file, ordered by the absolute value of the differential coverage statistic: trnCounts.wide <- arrange(trnCounts.wide, -abs(DCov)); write.csv(trnCounts.wide[,c("Transcript","WTfwd","WTrev","ρ0fwd","ρ0rev", "DCov","Gene","Description","Chr","Start","End")], file="wide_transcript_Counts_WTvsρ0.csv", row.names=FALSE); trnCounts.wide <- arrange(trnCounts.wide, -abs(DCov)); write.csv(trnCounts.wide[,c("Transcript","WTfwd","WTrev","ρ0fwd","ρ0rev", "DCov","Gene","Description","Chr","Start","End")], file="wide_transcript_Counts_WTvsρ0.csv", row.names=FALSE);
